first-release.comment: Public beta announced May 2025 at Google I/O (private preview Dec 2024); reached general availability Aug 6, 2025. Using the public-beta date per the month-year format.

status.comment: Generally available since Aug 6, 2025 (out of beta); actively shipping (skills, API, Gemini 3.x). Checked 2026-06-02.

license.comment: Closed-source Google product; offers a free introductory tier plus paid plans, hence freemium.

specialization.comment: Broad coding agent: bug fixing, feature building, version bumps, tests.

interface.comment: Primarily a web app; Jules Tools CLI/TUI added for terminal management of sessions.

host-ides.comment: Runs in the browser (web app) with a companion terminal CLI; not embedded in a desktop IDE.

open-source.comment: Proprietary Google product; the agent's own source is not openly licensed.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Asynchronous cloud agent: clones the repo into a Google Cloud VM and works remotely.

default-model-provider.comment: Powered exclusively by Google's Gemini models. As of 2026, Gemini 3.1 Pro is the default planner tier (Mar 2026) with Gemini 3 Flash as the base model for lighter work (Jan 2026); originally Gemini 2.5 Pro at GA.

model-choice.comment: Uses Gemini only; no documented user-facing switch between providers. Tier (free/Pro/Ultra) determines the Gemini version rather than free model choice.

bring-your-own-key.comment: No documented option to supply your own model API key; billing is via Google plans.

local-model-support.comment: Runs on Google Cloud VMs against Gemini; no local/self-hosted model option.

multi-file-editing.comment: Makes coordinated multi-file changes and shows a diff before opening a PR.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Plans, edits across files, installs deps, runs tests in a VM, and opens a PR with minimal per-step prompting.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Runs setup scripts, installs dependencies, and runs tests/commands in its Cloud VM (long-running processes like dev servers not supported).

mcp-support.comment: Official MCP server support shipped Feb 2, 2026 with hand-selected integrations (Linear, Neon, Supabase, Context7, Tinybird, Stitch); configured via Settings.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Supports multiple concurrent tasks (3 free / 15 Pro / 60 Ultra) running in parallel as independent cloud workers; the issue-triage skill dispatches parallel Jules agents to fix multiple issues.

codebase-indexing.comment: No documented semantic index/embeddings store; it clones the repo into a VM and loads a large chunk of it via Gemini's long context window rather than building an index. Left null since Google does not state whether a true index is built.

planning-mode.comment: Presents a plan and reasoning before executing; the user can modify the plan before/during/after.

multimodal-input.comment: Accepts image-based context in task descriptions (multimodal support).

browser-computer-use.comment: Can visualize web-app output but no documented capability to drive a real browser/GUI to verify its own changes.

operating-mode.comment: Fire-and-forget cloud agent: dispatch a task and it works asynchronously, then opens a PR. Plan can be reviewed but it is not an inline/interactive editor.

inline-completion.comment: No tab-style as-you-type completion; it is an async task agent.

background-async-execution.comment: Core design: tasks run asynchronously in the cloud while the developer does other work.

persistent-memory.comment: Per-repo memory (added Sep 30, 2025) saves preferences, nudges, and corrections across sessions; Jules also remembers user preferences over time and applies them to future tasks.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: No documented built-in checkpoint/rollback; changes land as a reviewable PR/branch the user can decline. Left null as the explicit feature is not described.

git-integration.comment: Deep GitHub workflow: clones repo, creates branches, opens pull requests, integrates GitHub Issues.

pr-creation.comment: Opens a pull request on completion with description, modified-file list, and design summary.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Automatically reads AGENTS.md at repo root (since Jun 20, 2025) for build/test commands and conventions; falls back to README.md for environment hints.

hooks-extensibility.comment: Public Jules API, Jules Tools CLI, a GitHub Action (jules-action), and MCP server integrations allow programmatic extension and scripting (e.g. triggering tasks from Slack or CI/CD).

permission-model.comment: Presents a plan for review/approval and changes land as a PR the user must merge; all execution runs in an isolated Cloud VM sandbox.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Google Cloud-hosted service only; no self-host/on-prem deployment.

privacy-mode.comment: Jules states it does not train on private repository content; privacy is a stated core principle.

enterprise-controls.comment: No dedicated Jules-specific enterprise governance (SSO/audit-logs/RBAC/VPC) documented in primary docs as of 2026-06-02; access is tied to Google accounts and Google AI Pro/Ultra plans. Left null rather than asserting none.

pricing-model.comment: Free introductory tier plus Google AI Pro and Ultra subscriptions.

free-tier.comment: Free introductory plan: 15 daily tasks, 3 concurrent tasks.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Cheapest paid plan is Google AI Pro at $19.99/mo (Ultra is $124.99/mo). A real free tier also exists. Checked 2026-06-02.

maturity.comment: GA since Aug 2025, backed by Google, large usage during beta (140k+ improvements); now has API, CLI, skills and Gemini 3.x, but still rapidly evolving, so 4 rather than 5.

community-size.comment: Broad Google reach and tens of thousands of beta tasks, but a newer single-vendor cloud agent; estimated medium relative to Copilot/Cursor scale.

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: MCP server integrations, a public API with community MCP servers, a Gemini CLI extension, and an Agent Skills library form a small but growing ecosystem.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md from the repo root (since Jun 20, 2025); also reads README.md for environment hints, but AGENTS.md is the dedicated instruction-file convention.

memory-scopes.comment: Per-repo (project) memory and AGENTS.md, plus user-level preferences Jules remembers over time across tasks. No documented per-folder or enterprise-policy scope.

scoped-rules-files.comment: Only a single always-applied AGENTS.md at the repo root is documented; no path/glob, file-type, or agent-requested conditional rule-file mechanism.

auto-memory.comment: Jules automatically saves preferences, nudges, and corrections per repo and applies them to future tasks (Sep 30, 2025); memory can be toggled on/off per repo in repo settings under 'Knowledge'.

skill-support.comment: Jules Agent Skills: reusable packaged capabilities defined as a SKILL.md plus scripts/resources, following the Agent Skills open standard (compatible with Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Antigravity) and working with the Jules SDK. Example: an automate-github-issues skill.

skill-invocation.comment: Skills follow the Agent Skills open standard: the agent selects and loads a skill autonomously when relevant (model-callable) and users install/trigger them (user-callable).

always-on-surface.comment: Agent Skills open standard uses progressive disclosure — the SKILL.md acts as 'Mission Control' with name/description loaded by default and the full skill body pulled in on invocation.

skill-distribution.comment: Skills are distributed via a public git repository (google-labs-code/jules-skills) and installed with a 'skills' CLI tool that detects active coding agents and places the skill files in the appropriate directory.
